prompt: Build a browser-based JWT decoder and inspector. Single HTML file, no external dependencies. Paste a JWT into a textarea and it instantly decodes the header and payload into formatted JSON with syntax highlighting. Show expiration status (expired/valid/no-exp) with a colored badge. Include a 'Copy payload' button. Add a signature section that shows the algorithm used and the raw signature in base64. Handle malformed tokens gracefully with clear error messages. Dark theme, monospace fonts for JSON output.

Build a browser-based Pomodoro timer with task tracking. Single HTML file, no external dependencies. Features: 25-min work / 5-min break / 15-min long break (every 4 cycles). Add a task input field where users type what they're working on. Show a circular progress ring that animates down. Play a subtle CSS-animated pulse when the timer ends (no audio needed). Track completed pomodoros in a session log below the timer showing task name, start time, and completion status. Include start/pause/reset buttons. Persist session log to localStorage. Clean, focused UI with large readable timer digits.

Build a browser-based drum machine / beat sequencer. Single HTML file, no external dependencies. 16-step sequencer grid with 4 instrument rows (kick, snare, hi-hat, clap). Use the Web Audio API to synthesize each sound (no samples). Tempo control slider (60-200 BPM). Play/stop button. Visual playhead that moves across the active step. Click grid cells to toggle beats on/off. Include a volume knob per row. Add a 'randomize' button that fills a random pattern. Neon-on-dark aesthetic with glowing active steps. The grid should be responsive and work on mobile widths.

Build a browser-based README generator. Single HTML file, no external dependencies. Provide a form with fields: project name, description, installation steps (textarea), usage example (textarea), license selector (dropdown: MIT, Apache-2.0, GPL-3.0, BSD-2), author name, and repo URL. A live preview pane renders the Markdown output in real-time as the user types (parse Markdown to HTML for the preview). Include sections: title with badge placeholders, description, installation, usage with code fencing, contributing boilerplate, and license. 'Copy Markdown' button copies the raw .md text. 'Download' button saves as README.md file. Split-pane layout: form on left, preview on right.

Build a browser-based HTTP status code reference. Single HTML file, no external dependencies. Show all standard HTTP status codes (1xx through 5xx) grouped by category with color-coded headers (blue=info, green=success, yellow=redirect, orange=client-error, red=server-error). Each code shows: number, name, and a one-line description. Include a search/filter box that instantly filters codes by number or name as you type. Click any code to expand an inline detail panel with a longer explanation and common causes. Add a 'copy as curl' button that copies a sample curl command returning that status. Responsive card grid layout. Include at least 40 status codes.

Build a browser-based Picross (nonogram) puzzle game. Single HTML file, no external dependencies. Generate a random 10x10 grid puzzle with row and column clues. Left-click fills a cell, right-click marks it as empty (X). Show numeric clues along the top and left side of the grid. Include puzzle validation: when the player solves it, show a success message with time taken. Add a 'new puzzle' button that generates a fresh random puzzle. Include an undo button (at least 10 levels). Timer display showing elapsed time. The generated puzzles must be solvable (the clue generation must match the hidden solution). Clean pixel-art inspired aesthetic.

Build a browser-based CSS unit converter and calculator. Single HTML file, no external dependencies. Input a value with a CSS unit (e.g., '16px', '1.5rem', '2em', '100vh', '50vw', '10pt', '2cm', '1in') and convert it to all other applicable units. Allow setting a base font-size (default 16px) and viewport dimensions (default 1920x1080) for accurate rem/em/vh/vw conversions. Show a results table with all conversions, each row copyable on click. Include a formula explanation below each conversion showing the math. Add a 'swap' feature to quickly convert between two specific units. Input validation with helpful error messages for unsupported units. Support chained calc() expressions like '16px + 1rem'.

Build a browser-based CSV data visualizer. Single HTML file, no external dependencies. Drag-and-drop a CSV file (or paste CSV text into a textarea) and render it as an interactive chart using Canvas 2D. Auto-detect numeric columns and offer chart type selection: bar, line, scatter, and pie. Let the user pick which column is X-axis and which is Y-axis from dropdowns. Show a data table preview of the first 20 rows below the chart. The chart must include: axis labels, gridlines, hover tooltips showing exact values, and a legend. Add a 'download chart as PNG' button using canvas.toDataURL(). Support files up to 10,000 rows without freezing. Color palette should auto-assign distinct colors per series. Responsive layout that works at 768px width.
